task_completion.description: Open the local_rules.xml file for editing
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Wazuh Dashboard UI showing the 'local_rules.xml' file open in the built-in XML editor, or a terminal showing the file open in a text editor.

task_completion.description: Add the rule block with the correct ID and severity level
task_completion.visual_evidence: XML code visible in the editor showing the opening tag: <rule id="100010" level="9">.

task_completion.description: Add the condition to trigger when rule 5710 fires
task_completion.visual_evidence: XML code visible inside the new rule block showing the tag: <if_sid>5710</if_sid>.
XML code visible inside the new rule block showing the tag: <description>Invalid SSH user detected - potential unauthorized access attempt</description>.

task_completion.description: Save the rule and verify it is in the custom rules list
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success notification in the Wazuh UI (e.g., 'File saved successfully' or a prompt to restart the manager), OR rule 100010 visibly listed in the Wazuh 'Custom rules' data table.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent interacted with the actual Wazuh Dashboard or a legitimate terminal session
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots should show the Wazuh web interface (Kibana/OpenSearch Dashboards) or a standard terminal window connected to the Wazuh manager, not a local desktop text editor like Notepad.

integrity.description: Ensure the rule was written with proper XML syntax
integrity.visual_evidence: The added rule must be properly enclosed in <rule>...</rule> tags and placed correctly within the parent <group>...</group> tags of the local_rules.xml file.

integrity.description: Ensure the success message or rule list was not faked using browser developer tools
integrity.visual_evidence: No browser developer tools (Elements tab, Console) should be open and actively modifying the DOM to fake the presence of the rule in the UI.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target Rule ID: 100010. Target Severity Level: 9. Parent SID (if_sid): 5710. Target Description: 'Invalid SSH user detected - potential unauthorized access attempt'.
